brief.headline: Aluminium is extending its rebound - the latest published LME official (22-Jul) settled higher again at cash $3,205 / 3M $3,185 with the backwardation steepening to +$20 and stock at a fresh 2022-low 276,775 t - as the shipping disruption widens to a SECOND waterway: Houthis struck two Saudi tankers in the Red Sea on 23-Jul, Brent broke $100, and the tabled ceasefire stalled, tilting the near-term skew back toward escalation.
bullets: What moved: the 22-Jul LME official settled higher for a second straight session (cash $3,205.00 / 3M $3,185.00; the 23-Jul official had not posted at this Friday-morning refresh), steepening the cash-3M backwardation to +$20.00 and clearing the $3,160 pivot.

The escalation: Yemen's Houthis struck two Saudi oil tankers in the Red Sea on 23-Jul - a second maritime front alongside a still-stalled Strait of Hormuz - and Brent broke above $100/bbl for the first time since May (Goldman flags $120+ in Q4 if it persists).

Still tight: LME stock at 276,775 t is a fresh low since 2022 (~-46% YTD) with SHFE also drawing; the steepening backwardation is the curve confirming physical tightness, not headline risk alone. Norsk Hydro's Q2 beat (22-Jul) shows the price/premium reaching producer margins.

The watch: the tabled 10-day ceasefire has stalled (Trump: Iran 'not ready yet'), so the de-escalation offset has receded. Offsets remain EGA's Al Taweelah alumina restart and record Chinese exports (June 711 kt). Watch the 23-Jul official when it posts, the $3,230/$3,260 resistance stack, and whether stock keeps drawing.

notes: The alumina market remains the mirror image of metal: globally LOOSENING (EGA's 2.4 Mtpa restart ramping since 10-Jul, Guinea bauxite oversupply, Chinese port stocks building; SMM Alumina Index easing toward ~Y2,730/t) - ex-China consolidation sits in the $300-330 band (FOB Australia $328, from ~$338).

The regional wrinkle widened again overnight: the shipping disruption spread to a SECOND waterway as Yemen's Houthis struck two Saudi oil tankers in the Red Sea on 23-Jul, on top of a still-stalled Strait of Hormuz. The war-risk cover embedded in the ~60% of Gulf alumina imports that transit Hormuz - and in Red Sea-routed reagent and alumina freight - is re-pricing wider, not fading. SMM separately flags ex-China metallurgical-grade output down ~6% y/y in June.

Guinea's pending export-quota policy (a considered cap near 150 Mt for 2026, vs 178 Mt shipped in 2025, targeting >$100/dmt bauxite) remains undecreed and is the one clear bullish catalyst on the commodity leg; record Australian and Metro Mining shipments underline how long the seaborne bauxite market currently is.

headlines.title: Yemen's Houthis struck two Saudi oil tankers (Encelia, Layla) in the Red Sea on 23-Jul, opening a SECOND maritime front alongside the effectively-closed Strait of Hormuz. Brent broke above $100/bbl for the first time since May (~+7% on the day). A 'serious escalation' that widens the shipping-risk zone for Gulf-region metal and reagent flows.

The tabled 10-day ceasefire has stalled: President Trump signalled Iran is 'not ready yet' and needs 'more of the same'. The de-escalation path that had kept the flat price two-sided has receded, shifting the near-term skew back toward escalation.

EGA's Al Taweelah refinery has been at full alumina production since 10-Jul, targeting 50% of capacity within days and full capability by end-2026. At 2.4 Mt in 2025 it supplies close to half of EGA's alumina need - the only material supply-side relief currently visible and the key reason the flat price is grinding rather than gapping.

LME stock is 276,775 t on the latest published 22-Jul official, a fresh low since 2022 and roughly 46% below the 509,250 t start-of-year level, with SHFE stockpiles also declining. Cash-3M has STEEPENED to a +$20.00 backwardation - prompt tightness, not surplus.
headlines.source: Westmetall / LME official series (22-Jul-2026, latest published)

headlines.title: Norsk Hydro's Q2 2026 (reported 22-Jul) beat: adjusted EBITDA NOK 8,923m (+14.5% y/y), Aluminium Metal EBITDA NOK 6,421m, adjusted EPS NOK 2.2 (from NOK 1.7), free cash flow NOK 4bn, 12-mo adjusted RoaCE 10.9% - and the shares rose. Higher aluminium prices and product premiums drove the beat. The cleanest public read that the disruption-quarter price/premium is flowing to producer margins.
headlines.source: Norsk Hydro Q2 2026 release; Business Recorder; StockTitan (22-Jul-2026)

Shares rose on the print. First European producer P&L of the Hormuz-disruption…

Net profit $200.3m (+316% YoY); EPS 53 fils; sales −17% on Gulf supply-chain disruption. LME averaged $3,195/t (+22% YoY) drove profit surge; volumes hit by regional conflict; ~19% capacity in controlled shutdown — restart path improves post-truce.
Net profit SAR 1.63bn (~$436m, +5.5% YoY); revenue SAR 8.79bn (+3.2%); EBITDA $964m. Strong pricing across commodities; partly offset by lower phosphate volumes; 2026 capex set >$4bn.

H1 copper-equivalent output +3% y/y; aluminium division improved; strong bauxite recovery; FY aluminium guidance UNCHANGED at 3.25–3.45 Mt; Pilbara Q2 shipments 89 Mt (+5%). First upstream print of the Q2 season (this morning): Western volumes are intact — the Gulf disruption is expressing itself in…

June-quarter production report: Worsley alumina 943 kt, Hillside aluminium 179 kt; group beat FY26 production guidance with quarterly sales +15%. Sale of the aluminium value chain to Alcoa (announced 01-Jul) progressing. Upstream volumes intact and above guidance; the aluminium/alumina divestment to…

Hindalco PAT ₹5,283cr (+66% YoY); India Al upstream EBITDA margin ~46%; Novelis FY26 adj. EBITDA $1.6bn (−9%). Record India operations; Novelis pressured by Oswego outage (~$104m) and tariffs (~$143m).
Net profit guided RMB 5.30–5.59bn (+50–58% YoY). Strong on higher Al prices; China output near self-imposed 45 Mt capacity cap.
Underlying profit $6.2bn (+>20%); underlying EBITDA $15.5bn (+25%, 58% margin); interim div 73c. Copper-led strength; broad base-metals read-across positive for the complex.
FY25 results; copper & aluminium average prices +9% YoY; zinc metal +4%. Resilient structural demand for Cu/Al cited; broadly constructive sector tone.
FY25 net loss $455m (vs $803m profit in 2024) on higher costs & lower output. Analysts expect stronger H1 2026 on higher prices; Asia pivot amid fractured trade flows.

Guides H1-26 net profit ¥1.8–1.95bn, +154.4–175.6% YoY. Credits industry-chain cost reduction, aluminium prices fluctuating at highs, high-value-added deep-processing mix and energy-saving retrofits — the first sizeable China H1 pre-announcement of the season and a positive read-across for Chinese p…

catalysts.text: Houthi attacks on two Saudi oil tankers in the Red Sea open a second maritime front; Brent breaks $100/bbl - a decisive escalation of the shipping-risk backdrop
The 23-Jul LME official, not yet posted at this morning refresh - the next settled print; watch whether the cash-3M backwardation steepens beyond +$20.00
LME warehouse stock report - whether draws continue below 276,775 t, a fresh low since 2022
EGA Al Taweelah ramp from 50% of capacity toward full capability by end-2026 - the principal supply-side offset in the current balance
Q4-2026 MJP CIF Japan premium negotiations open against the decade-high Q3 settlement of $395/t
Whether the stalled 10-day ceasefire revives or the conflict escalates further across Hormuz and the Red Sea - the single biggest swing factor, now tilted toward escalation

risks.text: Two-waterway escalation: a still-stalled Strait of Hormuz PLUS the 23-Jul Houthi attacks on Saudi tankers in the Red Sea keep roughly 9% of global primary aluminium supply at risk across two maritime chokepoints; a widening into general aluminium-bearing traff…
Inventory floor: LME stock at 276,775 t is a fresh low since 2022 and roughly -46% YTD with SHFE also drawing - there is no inventory buffer to absorb a fresh supply interruption

Energy cost squeeze: Brent above $100/bbl (Goldman flags $120+ in Q4) with EU gas following on a lag compresses marginal-producer margins in Europe and Asia and biases capacity toward curtailment rather than restart

Al Taweelah ramp disappoints or is interrupted - the alumina relief is the principal offset holding the flat price down and it is a ramp toward end-2026, not delivered tonnage, with its Gulf-routed output now under wider war-risk cover
Demand destruction: sustained elevated prices plus the 50% Section 232 rate on US imports encourage substitution and scrap displacement, particularly in packaging and construction

Diplomacy reversal: the stalled 10-day ceasefire could revive and reopen both waterways, collapsing the war-risk premium sharply (downside to price); a Section 232 rate change would likewise reprice the US premium complex quickly

caveats: Report refreshed Fri 24-Jul-2026 (morning London). The benchmark table and lme_series carry the latest PUBLISHED LME official - the 22-Jul settlement (cash $3,205.00 / 3M $3,185.00 / stock 276,775 t) from Westmetall, which had not yet posted the 23-Jul official at this refresh (officials print 13:20 London). The 22-Jul official was a real move higher; the 23-Jul official is the next settled read. Official settlements are the reference for the benchmark table.

MIXED SOURCE BASIS ON THE BOARD: the aluminium level on the metals board is set to the LME 22-Jul 3-month official (Westmetall, latest published) for a clean, consistent basis; copper is the latest LME 3M official (~$13,741/t, 23-Jul); nickel, zinc and lead are the latest publicly reported prints (Trading Economics/MetalCharts references, 23-Jul). Official and traded prices are different reference points and will not tie out exactly.

GEOPOLITICAL DEVELOPMENTS ARE FAST-MOVING and largely post-date any settled LME official. The 23-Jul Red Sea tanker attacks, the move above $100 in Brent, and the stalled ceasefire continue to evolve; treat the flat price as lagging the news flow, with the near-term skew now tilted toward escalation rather than two-sided.

PREMIUM ASSESSMENTS ARE PERIODIC, NOT DAILY: MJP settles quarterly, Rotterdam duty-unpaid and US Midwest are assessed by Platts/Fastmarkets on their own schedules. The figures shown are the latest PUBLIC prints (MJP Q3 settled $395/t on 06-Jul; EDU/US MW on 03-Jul). They are not daily marks.

ALL CONTENT IS PUBLIC MARKET INFORMATION for situational awareness only. No confidential, internal, company-specific or position data is included, and nothing here is trading, hedging or investment advice. Elliott Wave analysis is technical context, not a forecast or recommendation.
